steps.title: Identify the antecedent for the pronoun
steps.content: The blank is the object of "attributed," and it must refer back to "the wobble" (singular)—what the astronomers attributed to the gravitational influence.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Rule: the pronoun must be singular (matching "the wobble") and work as the direct object of "attributed ___ to the gravitational influence."

A — "each" "Each" is a determiner/pronoun requiring a noun after it ("each wobble") or referring to individual items in a set, but here there is only one wobble being attributed. Doesn't fit the structure. Fails.

B — "these" Plural demonstrative pronoun. "The wobble" is singular, so a plural pronoun creates an agreement error. Fails.

C — "it" Singular personal pronoun. Refers back to "the wobble" correctly, and "attributed it to the gravitational influence" is grammatically complete. Correct.

D — "them" Plural personal pronoun. Mismatches the singular antecedent "the wobble." Fails.

C is correct.
